theorem.class9.change_base.step1_detail: 📱 Aap ek calculator program kar rahay hain jismein sirf log₁₀ (common log) aur ln (natural log) buttons hain, lekin aapko log₂(8) compute karna hai. Ye wo classic problem hai jo Change of Base Law solve karta hai.
theorem.class9.change_base.step2_detail: Convert karein: m = b^x. Hum isay ek known base a ke terms mein express karna chahtay hain. Dono taraf log_a lene se: log_a m = log_a(b^x). Ab hum Power Law ko reverse mein use kar saktay hain!
theorem.class9.change_base.step3_detail: log_a m = x · log_a b. Exponent x samnay aa jata hai. Ab x ko isolate karein: x = log_a m / log_a b.
theorem.class9.change_base.step4_detail: Kyunki x = log_b m, hamein milta hai: log_b m = log_a m / log_a b. Toh log₂(8) = log₁₀(8) / log₁₀(2) = 0.9031 / 0.3010 = 3. Wakai, 2³ = 8.
theorem.class9.change_base.practice_ques: log₁₀(100) = 2. Change of base use kar ke log₂(100) find karein.
Equal chords → centre se equal distances.
Equal chords centre se equidistant hoti hain.
Equal chords centre se equidistant hoti hain
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.title: Theorem 9.5: Equidistant Chords Congruent Hoti Hain
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.final: Centre se equal distance par mojood chords length mein equal hoti hain.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.keyIn: Yeh "if and only if" ko complete karta hai: chords equal hain ↔ equidistant hain. Yeh symmetry circular grid design aur radial structures jese Ferris wheels mein fundamental hai.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.step1: Equal distance par chords draw karein
🎪 Do chords AB aur CD draw karein is tarah ke unke perpendicular distances centre se (OE aur OF) equal hon.
theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.step: ⭕ Centre O ke sath ek circle draw karein. Point B par circle ko touch karta hua ek tangent PQ draw karein. Radius OB draw karein.
Agar OB perpendicular NAHI hai, toh PQ par koi aur point O ke ziyada qareeb hoga bajaye B ke.
Wo qareeb point circle ke andar hoga, jiska matlab hai ke PQ circle ko do points par intersect karta hai — jo is baat ke khilaf hai ke PQ ek tangent hai.
Assumption ghalat hai, is liye OB ⟂ PQ. Radius aur tangent hamesha perpendicular hote hain.
theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.prac: Radius OB tangent ko B par milta hai. Unke darmiyan angle kya hai?
Tangent contact point par radius ke perpendicular hota hai.
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.title: Theorem 10.3: External Point se Do Tangents Equal Hote Hain
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.finalForm: Ek external point se circle tak do tangents ki lengths equal hoti hain.
theorem.class10.internal_touching.step1_: ⭕🔵 Ek bada circle (radius r₁) aur ek chota circle (radius r₂) uske andar draw karein, jo C par touch kar rahe hon.
theorem.class10.internal_touching.step2_: Circles C par touch karte hain, jo line AB par hai. Chota circle bade circle ke andar hai.
theorem.class10.internal_touching.step3_: Outer circle ke liye: AC = r₁. Inner circle ke liye: BC = r₂. Kyunki B andar hai: AB = r₁ − r₂.
theorem.class10.internal_touching.practi: 5 cm aur 2 cm radii wale do circles internally touch karte hain. Centres ke darmiyan distance kya hoga?
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.title: Theorem 10.6: Alternate Segment Theorem
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.finalF: Tangent aur chord ke darmiyan angle alternate segment mein angle ke barabar hota hai.
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.keyIns: Sailors is theorem ka istemal karte hain: ek coastline (tangent) aur lighthouse ki taraf ek line (chord) ke darmiyan angle unki position tay karta hai. Yeh circle geometry mein sab se khoobsurat theorems mein se ek hai!
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.step1_: 🎯 Ek circle draw karein jiska tangent PT point A par ho. Chord AB draw karein, jis se tangent aur chord ke darmiyan ∠APT banay.
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.step2_: Chord AB circle ko do segments mein divide karta hai. Alternate segment tangent ke opposite hota hai.
theorem.class10.alternate_segment.step3_: Tangent aur chord ke darmiyan angle alternate segment mein angle ke barabar hota hai.
theorem.class10.major_segment.title: Theorem 10.10: Major Segment mein Angle < 90°
theorem.class10.major_segment.finalFormu: Ek segment jo semicircle se bada ho, usme angle acute hota hai.
theorem.class10.major_segment.keyInsight: Theorems 10.11 ke sath mil kar, yeh circular segments mein angles ki complete classification deta hai. 90° par semicircle acute aur obtuse ke darmiyan boundary hai.
theorem.class10.major_segment.step1_labe: Chord AB draw karein jo major segment banaye
theorem.class10.major_segment.step1_deta: 📐 Chord AB draw karein jo ek major segment (semicircle se bada) banaye. Major arc par ek point P select karein.
theorem.class10.major_segment.step2_deta: Major segment dvara subtend kiya gaya central angle 180° se kam hota hai.
theorem.class10.major_segment.step3_deta: Kyunki central angle 180° se kam hai, inscribed angle 90° se kam (acute) hota hai.
theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.title: Complex Number ki Magnitude
theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.finalF: z = x + iy ki magnitude complex plane par origin se distance hoti hai.
theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.keyIns: Magnitude |z| ko modulus ya absolute value bhi kaha jata hai. Yeh measure karta hai ke complex number z = x + iy complex plane par origin se kitni door hai, bilkul distance formula ki tarah! Yeh signal processing mein fundamental hai jahan |z| ek signal ki amplitude ko represent karta hai.
theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.step1_: 🗺️ Aap ek radar operator hain jo ek circular radar screen par ek plane track kar rahay hain. Plane ki position (x, y) par hai — real part x east-west coordinate hai, aur imaginary part y north-south coordinate hai. Centre (origin) se distance range ke liye important hai.
theorem.class11.complex_inverse_polar.pr: |z⁻¹| = 1/2. Inverse ki magnitude reciprocal hoti hai.
theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.t: Polar Form mein Complex Numbers ki Division
theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.f: Magnitudes ko divide karein, angles ko subtract karein.
theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.k: Polar form mein complex division bohot elegant hai: magnitudes ko divide karein aur angles ko subtract karein! Yeh rectangular form se bohot asan hai aur AC power systems ke liye phasor analysis mein bohat istemal hota hai.
theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.s: Numerator aur denominator ko multiply karein
Trig identities use kar ke simplify karein

🔌 Ek electrical engineer ek power grid analyze kar raha hai. Do AC voltages z₁ = r₁(cos θ₁ + i sin θ₁) aur z₂ = r₂(cos θ₂ + i sin θ₂) hain. Unki ratio find karne ke liye, unhein polar form mein divide karein.
theorem.class11.determinant_identical.st: Row 1 aur Row 2 ko swap karein

📉 Aapka GPS receiver aapki position triangulate karne ke liye teen satellite signals (rows) use karta hai. Agar do satellites identical signals (identical rows) bhejte hain, toh receiver unhein distinguish nahi kar sakta — woh ek unique location find nahi kar sakta!

🎯 Agar hum Row 1 aur Row 2 swap karte hain, kyunki woh identical hain, matrix B matrix A ke identical ho jata hai. Lekin interchange property ke mutabiq, rows swap karne se determinant ka sign flip ho jata hai: |B| = −|A|.
🧮 Kyunki B = A (identical rows), |B| = |A|. Lekin |B| = −|A| bhi (interchange property se). Is liye |A| = −|A|.

✅ Dono sides par |A| add karein: 2|A| = 0 → |A| = 0. Yehi wajah hai ke Gauss-Jordan elimination break ho jata hai jab rows dependent hoti hain — determinant zero hona hamein batata hai ke matrix singular hai!
theorem.class11.determinant_identical.pr: Ek 3×3 matrix ki first aur second rows identical hain. det(A) kya hoga?
Identical rows → determinant zero hona chahiye
theorem.class11.triangle_midpoint.title: Triangle Midpoint Theorem
theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.title: Trapezium Midpoint Theorem
theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.final: Non-parallel sides ke midpoints ko jodne wala segment parallel sides ke sum ka aadha hota hai.
theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.keyIn: Bridge designers is theorem ko istemal karte hain: ek trapezoidal bridge girder ki midline top aur bottom widths ki average ke barabar hoti hai. Yeh numerical integration (trapezoidal rule) mein bhi istemal hota hai jahan endpoints ki average integral ko approximate karti hai!
theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.step1: 🌉 Aap ek civil engineer hain jo ek trapezoidal cross-section wala bridge design kar rahay hain. Top deck AB bottom base DC ke parallel hai. Aapko mid-span support beam ki length find karni hai.
theorem.class11.combination_formula.step: 🎯 Aap 5 volunteers mein se 3 logon ki team organize kar rahay hain. Aap pehle permutations (ordered arrangements) ke bare mein sochtay hain, lekin realize karte hain ke order matter nahi karta — Alice, Bob, Charlie ko pick karna aur Charlie, Bob, Alice ko pick karna ek hi team hai.
📐 r objects ki har combination ko r! different tareeqon se arrange kiya ja sakta hai. Toh permutations ki tadaad (ordered) = combinations ki tadaad × arrangements: ⁿPᵣ = X × r!.
🧮 Hum jantay hain ⁿPᵣ = n!/(n−r)!. Substitute karne par: X × r! = n!/(n−r)!.

✅ Dono sides ko r! se divide karein: X = ⁿCᵣ = n!/(r!(n−r)!). 5-choose-3 ke liye: ⁵C₃ = 5!/(3!2!) = 120/(6×2) = 10. 5 logon mein se 3 logon ki team bananay ke 10 possible tareeqay hain. Yeh binomial distribution aur Pascal's Triangle ki foundation hai!
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step2_d: 📐 Farz karein ke theorem n = k ke liye sach hai: (a+b)ᵏ = Σ ᵏCᵢ aᵏ⁻ⁱ bⁱ.
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step3_d: 🧮 k+1 ke liye prove karne ke liye, dono sides ko (a+b) se multiply karein: (a+b)ᵏ⁺¹ = (a+b)(ᵏC₀aᵏ + ᵏC₁aᵏ⁻¹b + ... + ᵏCₖbᵏ).
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step4_d: ✅ Distribute aur like terms group karne ke baad, coefficients Pascal ki identity ko follow karte hain: ᵏCᵣ₋₁ + ᵏCᵣ = ᵏ⁺¹Cᵣ. Is liye (a+b)ᵏ⁺¹ = Σ ᵏ⁺¹Cᵣ aᵏ⁺¹⁻ʳ bʳ. Induction ke zariye, yeh theorem tamam positive integers n ke liye sach hai!
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.practic: (a+b)⁴ mein a²b² ka coefficient find karein.
Pascal's triangle ki Row 4: 1, 4, 6, 4, 1. a²b² ka coefficient = ⁴C₂ = 6
Pascal's Triangle row n, position k use karein
theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.title: Fundamental Law of Trigonometry: cos(α−β)
theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.finalFo: Difference ka cosine = cosines ka product + sines ka product.
theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.keyInsi: Yeh akela formula tamam trigonometric identities ki maa hai! Is se, hum cos(α+β), sin(α±β), double-angle formulas, half-angle formulas, aur product-to-sum identities derive kar saktay hain. Yeh trigonometry ka Rosetta Stone hai.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step2_de: 🔄 Co-ratios use karte hue: sin(α+β) = cos(π/2−(α+β)) = cos[(π/2−α)−β] = cos(π/2−α)cos β + sin(π/2−α)sin β = sin α cos β + cos α sin β.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step3_de: 🧮 Let β = α in sin(α+β): sin(2α) = sin α cos α + cos α sin α = 2 sin α cos α. Cos ke liye: cos(2α) = cos²α − sin²α = 2cos²α − 1 = 1 − 2sin²α.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step4_de: 🔊 Audio engineers yeh istemal karte hain: sin(3α) = sin(2α+α) = sin(2α)cos α + cos(2α)sin α = (2 sin α cos α)cos α + (1 − 2 sin²α)sin α = 2 sin α(1−sin²α) + sin α − 2 sin³α = 3 sin α − 4 sin³α. Yeh synthesizers mein third harmonic generate karta hai!
theorem.class11.trig_identities.practice: sin(30°) = 0.5. Double-angle formula use kar ke sin(60°) find karein.
theorem.class12.power_rule.finalFormulaD: xⁿ ka derivative n·xⁿ⁻¹ hai kisi bhi positive integer n ke liye.
theorem.class12.power_rule.keyInsight: Power rule sab se ziyada use hone wala differentiation formula hai! Yeh polynomials ko simpler mein transform karta hai: har term ka exponent 1 se kam hota hai aur naya coefficient ban jata hai. Physics (velocity = position ka derivative) se le kar economics (total cost se marginal cost) tak har jagah use hota hai.
theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.s: 📐 Ek astronomer radius a wale circular telescope mirror ka area calculate kar raha hai. Integral ∫ √(a²−x²) dx appear hota hai. Substitute x = a sin θ, jo circle ki equation ko trigonometric form mein convert karta hai.
🧮 √(a²−x²) = √(a²−a²sin²θ) = a√(1−sin²θ) = a cos θ. Integral ban jata hai ∫ a cos θ · a cos θ dθ = a² ∫ cos²θ dθ.
📐 cos²θ = (1+cos 2θ)/2 use karein. Integrate karein: a²[θ/2 + sin 2θ/4] + C = (a²/2)θ + (a²/4) sin 2θ + C.

✅ Wapas substitute karne par: ∫√(a²−x²)dx = (a²/2)sin⁻¹(x/a) + (x√(a²−x²))/2 + C. Yeh formula height x ke circular segment ka area deta hai — civil engineering mein partially filled pipes mein water flow ke liye use hota hai!
theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.p: Formula use karein ∫ 1/√(a²−x²) dx = sin⁻¹(x/a) + C
theorem.class12.fundamental_theorem_calc: Definite integral bounds par antiderivative ke difference ke barabar hota hai.

FTC calculus ka sab se ahem theorem hai! Yeh do central concepts ko connect karta hai: differentiation aur integration inverse operations hain. Yehi wajah hai ke hum antiderivatives dhund kar areas compute kar saktay hain — geometry aur algebra ke darmiyan ek gehra talluq.

🏔️ Ek geologist river delta mein sediment ka volume calculate kar raha hai. Cross-sectional area shore se distance x ke sath vary karta hai. Define A(x) = ∫ₐˣ f(t) dt as the accumulated area from a to x.
📐 Definition se: A'(x) = lim[A(x+h)−A(x)]/h as h→0. Lekin A(x+h)−A(x) = ∫ₓˣ⁺ʰ f(t) dt ≈ f(x)·h Mean Value Theorem ke zariye. So A'(x) = f(x). Area function f ka ek antiderivative hai!
🧮 Kyunki A'(x) = f(x), f ka koi bhi antiderivative F, A se ek constant se differ karta hai: F(x) = A(x) + C. Phir F(b)−F(a) = A(b)−A(a).

✅ Lekin A(a) = ∫ₐᵃ f(t) dt = 0, toh F(b)−F(a) = A(b) = ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx. Definite integral bounds par evaluate kiye gaye antiderivative ke barabar hota hai. River delta volume: agar A'(x) = 2x (linear increase), toh x=0 se x=5 tak volume = [x²]₀⁵ = 25 cubic meters!
∫₁³ (2x) dx. F(3) − F(1) find karein jahan F(x) = x².
theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.fi: Triangle ki teeno altitudes hamesha ek point (orthocenter) par intersect karti hain.
theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.ke: Orthocenter teeno altitudes ka intersection point hai. Ek acute triangle mein yeh andar hota hai; obtuse triangle mein yeh bahar hota hai! Yeh concurrency wo wajah hai ke triangle ki teeno altitudes hamesha milti hain — surveying mein positions locate karne ke liye ek zabardast geometric fact.
theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.st: 🏔️ Ek surveyor zameen ka ek triangular plot map kar raha hai. Orthocenter find karne ke liye (jahan tamam lines of elevation milti hain), usay yeh prove karna hoga ke teeno altitudes ek point par intersect karti hain.
📐 BC ki slope (y₃−y₂)/(x₃−x₂) hai. Altitude AD ∦ BC, toh iski slope negative reciprocal hogi: m_AD = −(x₃−x₂)/(y₃−y₂).
🧮 Point A(x₁, y₁) ke sath point-slope form use karte hue: y − y₁ = m_AD(x − x₁). Rearrange karein: (x₃−x₂)x + (y₃−y₂)y − x₁(x₃−x₂) − y₁(y₃−y₂) = 0.

✅ BE aur CF ke liye bhi aisi hi equations likhein. Unke coefficients ko ek determinant mein rakhain. R₁ mein R₂ + R₃ add karne se first row zero ho jati hai (kyunki har row se coefficients ka sum cancel ho jata hai). Zero row ka matlab hai ke determinant = 0, jis se prove hota hai ke lines orthocenter par concurrent hain!
theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.pr: Triangle jiske vertices (0,0), (4,0), (2,5) hain. Teeno altitudes ek point par milti hain. Is property ko kya kehte hain?
Hint: Yeh "con" se shuru hota hai aur lines of elevation se related hai.
Sochein ke teen lines ka ek point par milna kya kehlata hai
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.fi: Triangle ki sides ke perpendicular bisectors circumcenter par intersect karte hain.
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.ke: Circumcenter us circle ka center hai jo teeno vertices se guzarta hai. Yeh teeno perpendicular bisectors ka intersection point hai. Yeh point teeno vertices se equidistant hota hai — jo wazeh karta hai ke aap kisi bhi teen non-collinear points se ek unique circle kyon draw kar sakte hain!
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.st: Simplify aur standard form mein likhein

📍 GPS triangulation: Teen satellite positions A, B, C maloom hain. Apni location dhundne ke liye, AB aur BC ke perpendicular bisectors construct karein — unka intersection point aapki position (circumcenter) hai!
📐 BC ka right bisector midpoint se guzarta hai aur BC ke perpendicular slope ke sath.
🧮 Multiply aur rearrange karne par standard linear equation aati hai. Note karein ke constant terms mein coordinates ke squares shamil hain.

✅ Teeno bisectors ke liye equations likhein. Coefficient determinant ki pehli row R₂ + R₃ add karne ke baad zero ho jati hai. Zero determinant = concurrency. Intersection point (circumcenter) teeno vertices se equidistant hota hai — circumscribed circle ka center!
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.pr: Circumscribed circle ke center ko kya kehte hain?
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.fina: Triangle ki teeno medians centroid par intersect karti hain — jo triangle ka center of mass hai.
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.keyI: Centroid har median ko vertex se midpoint tak 2:1 ratio mein divide karta hai. Yeh triangle ka center of mass hai — agar aap cardboard se ek triangle kat te hain, toh yeh centroid par perfectly balance hota hai! Engineers iska istemal triangular structures ka center of gravity maloom karne ke liye karte hain.
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.step: Median AD: vertex A se BC ke midpoint tak
⚖️ Ek architect bridge ki chhat ke liye ek triangular steel truss design kar raha hai. Center of mass (centroid) wo jagah hai jahan medians milti hain — yahan main support column lagaya jana chahiye.
📐 Median AD, A(x₁, y₁) aur BC ke midpoint se guzarti hai. Two-point formula ka istemal karte hue.
🧮 Cross-multiply karein aur ek standard linear form hasil karne ke liye simplify karein.

✅ Teeno median equations likhein. Rows add karne par unka coefficient determinant zero ho jata hai, jo concurrency prove karta hai. Intersection point G = ((x₁+x₂+x₃)/3, (y₁+y₂+y₃)/3) — centroid. Yeh vertices ki average bhi hai, jis se yeh center of mass ban jata hai!
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.prac: Triangle jiske vertices (0,0), (6,0), (3,6) hain. Centroid ka x-coordinate maloom karein.
theorem.class12.homogeneous_second_degre: Har homogeneous second-degree equation origin se guzarne wali lines ka ek pair represent karti hai.

Ek homogeneous equation ax² + 2hxy + by² = 0 ki tamam terms degree 2 ki hoti hain. Yeh hamesha do linear equations mein factor hoti hai, jiska matlab hai ke yeh origin se guzarne wali do seedhi lines ko represent karti hai. Agar h² − ab > 0 ho, toh lines distinct aur real hoti hain — optics mein light cone sections ko analyze karne ke liye istemal hota hai!
Dono sides ko a se multiply karein
Difference of squares ki tarah factor karein

🔦 Ek physicist point source se phailne wali light ki beam analyze kar raha hai. Light cone is homogeneous equation se describe hota hai: ax² + 2hxy + by² = 0. Maqsad yeh hai ke isay do mukhtalif rays mein factor kiya jaye.
📐 Main insight: h²y² add aur subtract kar ke ek perfect square complete karein.
🧮 Pehli teen terms (ax + hy)² banati hain. Baqi terms −y²(h²−ab) ban jati hain.

✅ Squares ke difference ke tor par: (ax + hy)² − (y√(h²−ab))² = 0. Yeh do linear equations mein factor hota hai: ax + y(h ± √(h²−ab)) = 0. Dono origin se guzarti hain (koi constant term nahi). Agar h² > ab: do distinct real lines. Agar h² = ab: ek repeated line. Agar h² < ab: complex (imaginary) lines.
Kya x² − y² = 0 do lines mein factor hota hai?
x² − y² = (x−y)(x+y) = 0 se lines x=y aur x=−y milti hain
Haan! x² − y² = (x−y)(x+y) = 0 origin se guzarne wali do lines hain: x = y aur x = −y.
theorem.class12.circle_general.title: Circle ki General Equation
theorem.class12.circle_general.finalForm: General equation center (−g, −f) aur radius √(g²+f²−c) walay circle ko represent karti hai.
theorem.class12.circle_general.keyInsigh: Square complete karne se, kisi bhi equation x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0 ko circle ke tor par likha ja sakta hai. Agar g²+f²−c > 0, toh yeh ek real circle hai; agar 0 ke barabar ho, toh yeh ek point circle hai; agar negative ho, toh equation ka koi real graph nahi hota. GPS receivers is tarah teen circle equations se aapki position maloom karte hain!
theorem.class12.circle_general.step2_lab: x aur y terms ko group karein
theorem.class12.circle_general.step3_lab: x aur y ke liye square complete karein
theorem.class12.circle_general.step1_det: 🗺️ Ek GPS receiver teen satellites se signals detect karta hai. Har satellite ka signal ek circle define karta hai: x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0. Receiver ko aapki position triangulate karne ke liye har circle ka center aur radius maloom karna hota hai.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step2_det: 📐 x aur y terms ko alag karein, aur c ko right side par layein.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step3_det: 🧮 g² add karein ((x+g)² complete karne ke liye) aur f² add karein ((y+f)² complete karne ke liye). Same side par g² aur f² subtract kar ke balance karein.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step4_det: ✅ Yeh standard circle form (x−h)² + (y−k)² = r² se match karta hai jahan h = −g, k = −f, r = √(g²+f²−c). Center (−g, −f) hai aur radius √(g²+f²−c) hai. Ek real circle ke liye, g²+f²−c > 0 zaroori hai!
theorem.class12.circle_general.practice_: x²+y²+4x+6y+4=0. Center ka x-coordinate maloom karein.
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.title: Circle ke Given Point par Tangent
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.finalForm: Point P(x₁, y₁) par circle ke tangent ki equation.
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.keyInsigh: Ek point par circle ka tangent us point par radius ke perpendicular hota hai. Formula x₁x + y₁y + g(x + x₁) + f(y + y₁) + c = 0 slope condition apply karke hasil hota hai. Isay "T = 0" ke naam se jana jata hai — x² → x₁x, y² → y₁y, x → (x+x₁)/2, y → (y+y₁)/2 se replace karein!
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step1_lab: Implicit differentiation se slope maloom karein
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step3_lab: Point P(x₁, y₁) par point-slope form
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step1_det: 🎯 Ek machinist ko gear tooth cut karna hai jo circular gear par ek precise point par contact kare. Cutting tool ko contact point par tangent line follow karni chahiye.
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step2_det: 📐 Implicitly differentiate karein: 2x + 2y·y' + 2g + 2f·y' = 0. y' ke liye solve karein: dy/dx = −(x+g)/(y+f).
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step3_det: 🧮 Point P(x₁, y₁) par, dy/dx mein substitute karein: slope m = −(x₁+g)/(y₁+f). Point-slope form mein tangent line: y − y₁ = m(x − x₁).
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step4_det: ✅ Cross-multiply aur rearrange karein. Result yeh elegant T = 0 formula hai: replace x² → x₁x, y² → y₁y, 2gx → g(x+x₁), 2fy → f(y+y₁), c → c. Tangent equation directly baghair derivative compute kiye likhi jati hai!
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.practice_: Circle x²+y²=25 par point (3,4). Tangent slope = −x₁/y₁ = −3/4 = ?
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.title: Parabola ki Standard Equation
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.finalF: Ek parabola jiska focus (a, 0) par ho aur directrix x = −a ho.
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.keyIns: Parabola un points ka set hai jo ek fixed point (focus) aur fixed line (directrix) se equidistant hotay hain. Yeh reflective property wajah hai ke parabolic mirrors parallel light rays ko focus par jama karte hain — jo telescopes, satellite dishes, aur solar cookers mein istemal hotay hain!
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step1_: Definition: focus tak distance = directrix tak distance
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step2_: Focus (a, 0) par aur directrix x = −a par rakhein
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step1_: 📡 Ek satellite dish antenna ki shakal parabolic hoti hai. Axis ke parallel aane walay signals focus par reflect hotay hain jahan receiver rakha jata hai. Definition: parabola par har point P focus F aur directrix line se equidistant hota hai.
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step2_: 📐 Coordinate axes aise chunein ke focus F = (a, 0) aur directrix x = −a ho. P(x,y) se F ka distance √[(x−a)²+y²] hai. Directrix ka distance |x−(−a)| = |x + a| hai.
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step3_: 🧮 Dono sides ko square karein (dono non-negative hain): (x−a)² + y² = (x+a)².
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step4_: ✅ Expand karein: x²−2ax+a²+y² = x²+2ax+a². Dono taraf se x² aur a² cancel karein. Solve karein: y² = 4ax. Yeh right side open hone walay parabola ki standard equation hai. Satellite dishes ke liye: receiver focus (a, 0) par hota hai aur dish y² = 4ax ko follow karti hai!
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.practi: y² = 4ax. 4a = coefficient se a ke liye solve karein.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.title: Parabola ka Tangent
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.finalFo: Tangency ki condition: y = mx + c ko y² = 4ax mein substitute karein aur discriminant = 0 set karein.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.keyInsi: Line y = mx + c tabhi tangent hoti hai y² = 4ax par agar c = a/m ho. Iska matlab hai ke tangent ki slope form y = mx + a/m hai. Kisi di hui slope m ke liye, theek do parallel tangents hoti hain (ek parabola ki har side par), siwaye vertex par vertical tangent ke!
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step1_l: Line ko parabola mein substitute karein
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step2_l: x ki quadratic form mein expand karein
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step3_l: Tangency ke liye discriminant = 0 set karein
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step1_d: 🎯 Ek architect ek parabolic arch design kar raha hai aur usay support beam angle calculate karne ke liye ek point par tangent line maloom karni hai. Line ki equation y = mx + c ko parabola y² = 4ax mein substitute karein.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step2_d: 📐 Expand karein: m²x² + 2mcx + c² = 4ax. Rearrange karein: m²x² + 2(mc−2a)x + c² = 0. Yeh x mein ek quadratic equation hai.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step3_d: 🧮 Tangency ke liye, line ko sirf ek point par intersect karna chahiye. Iska matlab hai ke quadratic ki ek double root hogi: iska discriminant D = b²−4ac zero hona chahiye.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step4_d: ✅ Expand karein: 4(m²c²−4amc+4a²) − 4m²c² = 0 → −16amc + 16a² = 0 → mc = a → c = a/m. Tangent equation slope form mein: y = mx + a/m. Yeh formula architects ko parabolic arch par kisi bhi point par support beam angle maloom karne mein madad karta hai!
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.practic: Parabola y² = 8x. Line slope m = 2. Tangency ke liye c maloom karein (c = a/m).
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.title: Ellipse ki Standard Equation
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.finalFo: Ek ellipse jiske foci (±c, 0) par hon aur c² = a²−b².
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.keyInsi: Ellipse aise points ka set hai jahan do foci tak distance ka sum constant (2a) hota hai. Yehi wajah hai ke planetary orbits elliptical hotay hain jahan Sun ek focus par hota hai (Kepler's First Law). Flattening eccentricity e = c/a = √(1−b²/a²) par depend karti hai!
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step1_l: Definition: foci tak distance ka sum = 2a
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step2_l: Foci (±c, 0) aur point P(x, y) set karein
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step3_l: Isolate karein aur do martaba square karein
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step4_l: Standard form ke liye b² = a²−c² rakhein
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step1_d: 🌍 Johannes Kepler ne daryaft kiya ke planets Sun ke gird elliptical raston par ghoomtay hain. Sun ek focus par hai. Ellipse par kisi bhi point se do foci tak distance ka sum hamesha constant hota hai — major axis ki length.
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step2_d: 📐 Foci F₁(−c, 0) aur F₂(c, 0) par rakhein. Ellipse par kisi bhi point P(x,y) ke liye: √[(x+c)²+y²] + √[(x−c)²+y²] = 2a.
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step3_d: 🧮 Ek radical ko isolate karein, dono sides ka square karein, dusre radical ko isolate karein, dobara square karein. Simplify karne ke baad: (a²−c²)x² + a²y² = a²(a²−c²).
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step4_d: ✅ Define karein b² = a²−c². Phir b²x² + a²y² = a²b² → x²/a² + y²/b² = 1. Earth ke orbit ke liye: a ≈ 149.6 million km, b ≈ 149.5 million km (taqreeban circular, e ≈ 0.017). Mars ki e ≈ 0.093 hai — wazeh tor par ziyada elliptical!
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.practic: Ellipse x²/25 + y²/9 = 1. c (center se focus ka distance) maloom karein.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.title: Hyperbola ki Standard Equation
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.final: Ek hyperbola jiske foci (±c, 0) par hon aur c² = a²+b².
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.keyIn: Hyperbola un points ka set hai jahan do foci tak distance ka difference constant (2a) hota hai. Ellipse ke bar-aks (jahan sum constant hota hai), hyperbola ki do alag branches hoti hain. Navigation (LORAN systems) mein istemal hota hai jahan do stations se signals pahunchne mein time difference ship ki location batata hai!
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step1: Definition: foci tak distance ka difference = 2a
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step2: Foci (±c, 0) aur point P(x, y) set karein
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step3: Do martaba square karein (ellipse jesa process lekin sign farq)
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step4: Standard form ke liye b² = c²−a² rakhein
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step1: 🚢 Ek ship LORAN navigation istemal karti hai: F₁ aur F₂ par do radio stations synchronized signals bhejte hain. Ship unke aane ke time ka difference measure karti hai. Constant time difference ka locus ek hyperbola hota hai jiske foci stations par hain.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step2: 📐 F₁(−c,0) aur F₂(c,0) tak distance ka absolute difference 2a hai. Wo branch chunein jahan PF₁ > PF₂ ho.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step3: 🧮 Ellipse wali double-squaring technique. Lekin sign ka farq (subtraction vs addition) ek different final form ki taraf le jata hai.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step4: ✅ Define karein b² = c²−a². Standard form x²/a² − y²/b² = 1 ki do branches hoti hain. Asymptotes: y = ±(b/a)x. LORAN ship: agar stations 100km door hain (2c=100) aur constant difference 60km hai (2a=60), toh b² = 50²−30² = 1600, aur ship x²/900 − y²/1600 = 1 par hai!
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.pract: Hyperbola x²/16 − y²/9 = 1. c (focus ka center se distance) maloom karein.
